meta.title: MS-FAE-1: Fabric Lakehouse, DirectLake, Semantic Models and Governance

domains.name: SQL Analytics Endpoint and Lakehouse SQL

questions.stem: Which Microsoft Fabric feature provides a single, tenant-wide logical data lake for Fabric workloads?

options.explanation: A Data Warehouse is a single analytical item type within Fabric, not the tenant-wide storage layer that underpins all experiences.
OneLake is the storage foundation shared across all Fabric workloads — one lake per tenant that every experience reads from and writes to.
Dataflows Gen2 is a transformation tool that reads from and writes to storage; it does not act as the underlying storage layer itself.
Deployment pipelines are a release-management feature for promoting Fabric content between development, test, and production stages.

questions.reference: [OneLake, the unified data lake - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-overview)

questions.stem: A team wants to expose files stored in Amazon S3 inside Fabric without copying them into OneLake first. Which feature should they use?

options.explanation: DirectLake is a semantic model storage mode that reads from OneLake-backed Delta files; it is not a mechanism for virtualizing external storage locations.
OneLake shortcuts let Fabric reference external storage such as ADLS Gen2 and Amazon S3 without copying data, providing a virtual path inside OneLake that reads directly from the source.
Deployment pipeline rules control parameter substitution when content is promoted between stages; they do not create storage access paths.
A KQL database stores event and log data inside an Eventhouse; it does not virtualize or reference S3 files.

questions.reference: [Unify data sources with OneLake shortcuts - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-shortcuts)

questions.stem: What is the main difference between a Fabric Lakehouse and a Fabric Data Warehouse?

options.text: Lakehouses support only CSV files while warehouses support Delta tables
options.explanation: Lakehouses are not restricted to CSV; they natively use Delta format and support other file types in the Files section.

options.text: Lakehouses are Spark-first over Delta files, whereas warehouses provide a SQL data warehousing experience
options.explanation: A Lakehouse centers on Delta files and Spark-based engineering workflows while the Data Warehouse is the SQL-centric experience for dimensional warehousing and T-SQL DDL/DML.
Fabric Warehouses do support transactional behavior and ACID semantics for their managed tables.

options.text: Lakehouses do not create a SQL endpoint
options.explanation: Every Fabric Lakehouse automatically exposes a SQL analytics endpoint that lets analysts query managed tables with read-only T-SQL.

questions.reference: [What is a lakehouse? - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview)

questions.stem: What is the SQL analytics endpoint of a Fabric Lakehouse primarily used for?

options.text: Running read-only T-SQL queries over lakehouse tables
options.explanation: The SQL analytics endpoint exposes the lakehouse's managed Delta tables as read-only SQL objects, letting analysts query them with T-SQL without running Spark.
Spark notebooks run on the Fabric Spark compute engine, not through the SQL analytics endpoint.
Capacity and workspace settings are managed in the Fabric admin portal, not via the SQL endpoint.
Publishing Power BI apps is done through the Power BI service workspace, not through a lakehouse SQL endpoint.

questions.reference: [What is the SQL analytics endpoint for a lakehouse? - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-sql-analytics-endpoint)

questions.stem: A Fabric pipeline must retry a copy operation automatically after transient failures. Which pipeline capability addresses this requirement most directly?

options.explanation: Workspace roles control which users can view or edit workspace content; they have no influence over pipeline execution behavior.
Configuring the retry count and interval on a pipeline activity tells the engine to automatically re-attempt that step when it encounters a transient failure, without any manual intervention.
DirectLake framing is a concept related to how a DirectLake semantic model loads column data for queries; it is unrelated to pipeline fault tolerance.
Endorsement labels (Promoted, Certified) are governance metadata for discoverability; they do not affect pipeline execution or retry logic.

questions.reference: [Pipeline Overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: Which Fabric item uses Power Query M as its primary transformation language?

options.explanation: Spark job definitions run code written in PySpark, Scala, or R — not Power Query M.
Dataflows Gen2 are built on Power Query and use M as the underlying transformation language, providing a low-code authoring experience that generates M behind the scenes.
Warehouse stored procedures use T-SQL as their language, not M.
Real-Time Dashboards are a visualization surface that queries Eventhouse data with KQL; they do not transform data using M.

questions.reference: [Differences between Dataflow Gen1 and Dataflow Gen2 - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: A semantic model should keep the logic reusable across many reports and avoid re-creating the same aggregation in every visual. What should the team create?

options.explanation: Visual calculations are scoped to a single visual in one report; they cannot be reused across other reports or other visuals in the same model.
OneLake shortcuts expose storage paths and do not define business calculation logic.
Measures centralize reusable business logic in the semantic model so every report that connects to the model can consume the same calculation without duplicating code.
A workspace identity is a security principal used for service authentication, not a DAX calculation artifact.

questions.reference: [Learn DAX basics in Power BI Desktop - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-quickstart-learn-dax-basics)

questions.stem: A report must read the newest parquet data directly from OneLake with minimal latency and without importing data into VertiPaq. Which storage mode fits best?

options.explanation: Import mode requires loading all data into VertiPaq's in-memory columnar store before queries can run, which directly contradicts the requirement to avoid importing.
DirectQuery translates every DAX query into a SQL (or equivalent) query sent to the source system, rather than reading parquet files from OneLake directly.
DirectLake reads column data directly from OneLake-backed Delta/parquet files on demand, providing near-import speed without a scheduled import process or full DirectQuery translation overhead.
A live connection targets an external Power BI semantic model or an Azure Analysis Services instance — it does not read parquet files from OneLake.

questions.reference: [Direct Lake overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/directlake-overview)

questions.stem: Which behavior should an analytics engineer expect when a DirectLake semantic model cannot stay in DirectLake mode for a query?

options.explanation: Hitting a DirectLake guardrail does not always cause an immediate query failure; Fabric is designed to gracefully handle these cases through fallback rather than hard errors.

options.text: Fabric falls back to DirectQuery for supported scenarios
options.explanation: When a DirectLake query path is blocked by a guardrail condition — such as an unsupported feature or row-level security — Fabric automatically falls back to DirectQuery behavior for that query.

options.text: The report silently switches to Import mode permanently
options.explanation: The semantic model is not permanently converted to Import mode; the storage mode definition of the model remains DirectLake and only individual queries fall back.

options.text: The dataset is deleted and recreated
options.explanation: Datasets are not deleted or recreated as a consequence of a fallback event.

questions.reference: [Direct Lake overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/directlake-overview)

questions.stem: What is the purpose of endorsement labels such as Promoted and Certified in Fabric and Power BI?

options.explanation: Endorsement is a discoverability and trust feature; encryption at rest is handled separately by the platform's storage layer.

options.text: To indicate trusted, governed content to users
options.explanation: Endorsement labels help users distinguish curated, trustworthy content (Promoted and Certified) from unverified items when browsing the data hub or connecting to semantic models.

options.text: To allocate more capacity units automatically
options.explanation: Endorsement does not influence capacity unit allocation; it is purely a metadata signal.
Endorsement complements but never replaces row-level security, which filters data rows based on user identity.

questions.reference: [Promoted and certified items](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-endorsement-overview)

questions.stem: A semantic model should hide a sensitive salary column from report builders while keeping other columns in the same table available. Which control is most appropriate?

options.explanation: Row-level security (RLS) filters which rows a user can see based on role predicates; it does not hide individual columns or table objects.
Object-level security (OLS) can restrict access to specific columns or entire tables, making them invisible and unusable to users or roles that are not granted access.
Deployment pipeline rules adjust parameter values when content is promoted between stages; they are not a data access control mechanism.
V-order is a storage write optimization that improves read performance for Power BI engine workloads; it has no security implications.

questions.reference: [Object-Level Security (OLS) with Power BI - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/security/service-admin-object-level-security)

questions.stem: A regional manager should see only rows for their own sales region in every report that uses the same semantic model. Which feature should be implemented?

options.explanation: Object-level security hides entire tables or columns from users; it does not filter which rows within a table a specific user can see.
Row-level security (RLS) uses DAX filter expressions to restrict which rows are visible at query time based on the authenticated user's identity or role membership.
Query folding is a Power Query optimization that pushes transformation steps to the source system; it is unrelated to per-user row filtering in a semantic model.
Deployment pipeline rules control how item parameters differ between dev/test/prod stages; they do not implement data row filtering.

questions.reference: [Row-level security (RLS) with Power BI - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/service-admin-rls)

questions.stem: Which capability promotes content from development to test to production while preserving stage separation in Fabric?

options.explanation: Deployment pipelines are the Fabric ALM feature that moves items such as semantic models, reports, and lakehouses between linked development, test, and production workspace stages.
OneLake shortcuts virtualize references to external or cross-workspace storage; they do not promote or migrate workspace items between environments.
The staging lakehouse in Dataflows Gen2 is a temporary intermediate location used during dataflow execution, not a release-management stage.
SQL analytics endpoints provide read-only T-SQL access to lakehouse tables; they are query surfaces, not deployment tools.

questions.reference: [Overview of Fabric deployment pipelines - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/cicd/deployment-pipelines/intro-to-deployment-pipelines)

questions.stem: What does the Fabric Capacity Metrics app help an analytics engineer monitor?

options.text: DAX syntax errors inside a single report
options.explanation: The Capacity Metrics app shows resource consumption trends across the whole capacity; DAX debugging is done in tools such as DAX Studio or Performance Analyzer.

options.text: Capacity utilization, throttling, and workload consumption
options.explanation: The app surfaces CU utilization over time, identifies throttled operations, and shows which Fabric workloads are consuming the most resources.
Workspace membership is managed in workspace settings, not through the Capacity Metrics app.
The app covers all Fabric workloads — Data Engineering, Data Warehousing, Power BI, and more — not just SQL endpoint indexes.

questions.reference: [What is the Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app? - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/enterprise/metrics-app)

questions.stem: A team needs analysts to query curated Delta tables with T-SQL but data engineers still want notebook access to the same data. Which Fabric design best meets both needs?

options.text: Store the data only in an Eventhouse
options.explanation: Eventhouse is optimized for real-time KQL workloads over event-style data; it is not the natural home for curated Delta tables used by SQL analysts and Spark engineers.

options.text: Use a Lakehouse so Spark notebooks and the SQL analytics endpoint can both access the tables
options.explanation: A Lakehouse automatically provides a Spark notebook interface for engineers and a SQL analytics endpoint for analysts, allowing both personas to work against the same underlying Delta tables.

options.text: Use only a Power BI dataflow
options.explanation: A Power BI dataflow is a transformation layer that lands data somewhere else; it is not itself the shared analytical store that both Spark and SQL consumers would use.

options.text: Use a Warehouse because notebooks cannot work with Lakehouses
options.explanation: Spark notebooks do work with Fabric Lakehouses — that is one of the primary use cases for the Lakehouse item.

questions.reference: [What is a lakehouse? - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview)

questions.stem: Which Fabric orchestration item is best for sequencing copy, notebook, and stored procedure activities?

options.explanation: A semantic model serves BI analytical queries using DAX; it does not orchestrate data movement or execution steps.
Data pipelines are the Fabric orchestration surface that lets you chain Copy Data, Notebook, Stored Procedure, and other activities into a sequenced workflow.
DirectLake is a storage mode for semantic models, not a pipeline orchestration artifact.
A KQL query set stores saved KQL queries for Eventhouse data exploration; it does not orchestrate multi-step data workflows.

questions.reference: [Pipeline Overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: When should an analytics engineer prefer a calculated column over a measure?

options.text: When the value should aggregate dynamically by filter context only
options.explanation: Dynamic filter-context aggregation is the primary use case for measures, not calculated columns.

options.text: When the logic must run only inside a pipeline
options.explanation: Pipelines execute data movement and transformation logic; DAX calculated columns live in the semantic model and have no relationship to pipeline execution.
DirectLake is a storage mode and does not dictate whether you should use a calculated column or a measure for business logic.

options.text: When the value must be evaluated once per row and stored in the model
options.explanation: Calculated columns are row-context DAX expressions that are evaluated once per row during table refresh and stored in the model's compressed columnar cache, making them suitable for values that depend on row-level attributes.

questions.reference: [Learn DAX basics in Power BI Desktop - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-quickstart-learn-dax-basics)

questions.stem: What is the primary benefit of incremental refresh in a large Fabric semantic model?

options.text: It disables partitioning to simplify refresh
options.explanation: Incremental refresh relies on partitioning — it creates date-range partitions and processes only the ones that fall within the refresh window; it does not disable partitioning.

options.text: It refreshes only recent partitions instead of reprocessing all historical data
options.explanation: Incremental refresh reduces processing time and resource consumption by refreshing only the partitions covering recent data periods, while leaving historical partitions intact.

options.text: It forces every visual to use DirectQuery
options.explanation: Incremental refresh is an Import-mode optimization; it does not force visuals to use DirectQuery.

options.text: It replaces the need for a date table
options.explanation: A date dimension table is still recommended and remains useful for DAX time intelligence calculations regardless of whether incremental refresh is enabled.

questions.reference: [Configure incremental refresh and real-time data for Power BI semantic models - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/incremental-refresh-overview)

questions.stem: A model contains two relationships from `Sales` to `Date`, one for order date and one for ship date. Which modeling pattern best describes this?

options.explanation: A bridge table is used to resolve many-to-many relationships between two fact or dimension tables; it does not describe the same dimension being joined multiple times for different date types.
A slowly changing dimension (SCD) describes how historical attribute changes are tracked over time within a dimension table, not the reuse of a dimension in multiple relationship roles.

When a single dimension table — here, Date — is joined to the same fact table more than once to serve different analytical purposes (order date vs. ship date), the pattern is called a role-playing dimension.
A degenerate dimension is a dimensional attribute stored on the fact table itself (such as an invoice number) rather than in a separate dimension table.

questions.reference: [Understand star schema and the importance for Power BI - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/guidance/star-schema)

questions.stem: A pipeline loads on-premises SQL Server data into Fabric, but the source is not publicly reachable. What additional component is typically required?

options.explanation: Object-level security is a semantic model feature that restricts which tables and columns are visible to users; it plays no role in establishing connectivity to on-premises data sources.
An on-premises data gateway runs on a machine inside the private network and creates a secure bridge between cloud-managed Fabric services and on-premises or private-network data sources.
DirectLake framing is an internal mechanism for loading column segments from OneLake; it is not a connectivity component for bridging on-premises networks.
OneLake shortcut policies govern data access to shortcut targets; they do not provide network-level connectivity from Fabric to private on-premises systems.

questions.reference: [Differences between Dataflow Gen1 and Dataflow Gen2 - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: Which statement about OneLake shortcuts is accurate?

options.text: They always duplicate source files into Fabric-managed storage
options.explanation: Shortcuts do not copy or duplicate data; they create a virtual reference that Fabric resolves to the actual location at read time.

options.text: They can be created only for Fabric Warehouses
options.explanation: Shortcuts can be created within Lakehouses and other Fabric items, not only within Data Warehouses.

options.text: They require DirectQuery in Power BI
options.explanation: Shortcuts are independent of Power BI storage mode; a DirectLake or Import model can read from data exposed via a shortcut.

options.text: They provide a metadata link to data in another location
options.explanation: A OneLake shortcut is a metadata pointer — it tells Fabric where to find data (another OneLake path, ADLS Gen2, Amazon S3, etc.) without copying the data into a new location.

questions.reference: [Unify data sources with OneLake shortcuts - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-shortcuts)

questions.stem: A DirectLake model is showing poor performance because required columns are not being loaded efficiently into memory. Which Fabric concept is most closely related to how DirectLake reads column segments?

options.explanation: Snapshot isolation is a database transaction-concurrency concept; it describes how readers see a consistent view of data without blocking writers, not how DirectLake loads columnar data.
Gateway clustering relates to high-availability configuration of on-premises data gateways; it has nothing to do with DirectLake column loading.
DirectLake framing is the process by which the engine identifies and loads the column segments needed for a query from OneLake-backed parquet/Delta files into the VertiPaq cache.
Cross-filter direction is a relationship property in the semantic model that controls how filters propagate between tables; it does not govern how data is read from storage.

questions.reference: [Direct Lake overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/directlake-overview)

questions.stem: Which choice best supports governed self-service reuse of curated logic across many reports?

options.text: Embed every calculation separately in each report visual
options.explanation: Duplicating calculation logic in individual report visuals leads to inconsistent metrics, makes maintenance difficult, and undermines governance.
A notebook-only workflow produces processed data but does not provide a governed BI semantic layer with measures, relationships, and security that report builders can reuse.

options.text: Store only CSV exports in OneLake
options.explanation: Storing CSV exports in OneLake provides raw data but no business logic, relationships, or security; report builders would still need to re-create every calculation.
A shared semantic model centralizes measures, relationships, and security definitions in one place, so any number of reports can connect to the same model and consume consistent, governed logic.

questions.reference: [Semantic model modes in the Power BI service - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: A Fabric administrator wants users to discover trusted datasets quickly while preserving strict access controls separately. Which combination is most appropriate?

options.text: Use endorsement instead of security roles
options.explanation: Endorsement communicates curation quality to users; it is not a security mechanism and cannot replace role-based or row/object-level access controls.

options.text: Use workspace roles only and avoid semantic-model security
options.explanation: Workspace roles grant broad access to all items in a workspace; they are typically too coarse to implement the fine-grained data restrictions needed for sensitive semantic models.

options.text: Use DirectLake fallback rules as a governance control
options.explanation: DirectLake fallback is a runtime query behavior — it determines how a query executes when DirectLake guardrails are hit; it is not a governance or access-control mechanism.

options.text: Use endorsement for trust signaling and RLS/OLS for access control
options.explanation: Using endorsement labels (Promoted/Certified) makes trusted content discoverable, while RLS filters rows and OLS restricts columns/tables for the appropriate audiences — both layers work together without overlap.

questions.reference: [Promoted and certified items](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-endorsement-overview)

questions.stem: Why might a team choose Dataflows Gen2 instead of a Spark notebook for a simple source-to-target cleansing process?

options.text: Dataflows Gen2 are the only way to schedule refreshes
options.explanation: Scheduling in Fabric is not exclusive to Dataflows Gen2; pipelines and Spark job definitions can also be scheduled.

options.text: Spark notebooks cannot read files in OneLake
options.explanation: Spark notebooks can read from and write to OneLake using the standard file system paths or the mssparkutils API.
Dataflows Gen2 are ETL tools that prepare and load data; they do not replace semantic models, which serve analytical queries.

options.text: Dataflows Gen2 provide a low-code Power Query authoring experience
options.explanation: Dataflows Gen2 expose a visual Power Query editor that lets business analysts or less experienced engineers perform cleansing steps without writing Spark code.

questions.reference: [Differences between Dataflow Gen1 and Dataflow Gen2 - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: An analyst needs to query the same lakehouse tables with familiar T-SQL syntax but should not modify the underlying data. Which endpoint should the analyst use?

options.explanation: A Fabric Warehouse endpoint is a separate item that supports full T-SQL DML — the analyst could modify data there, which violates the read-only requirement.
The lakehouse SQL analytics endpoint exposes the lakehouse's managed Delta tables as read-only T-SQL objects; analysts can SELECT but cannot INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE.
The Spark monitoring UI is used to inspect and troubleshoot Spark job executions, not to query data.
The Capacity Metrics app monitors CU utilization and workload consumption; it does not provide a SQL query surface over lakehouse tables.

questions.reference: [What is the SQL analytics endpoint for a lakehouse? - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-sql-analytics-endpoint)

questions.stem: Which semantic model storage mode typically provides the fastest interactive performance after data is fully loaded into memory?

options.explanation: DirectQuery translates every DAX query into a native source query at runtime; performance is bounded by source query latency and concurrency limits.
A live connection delegates query execution to an external semantic model or Analysis Services instance and does not keep data locally in memory.
Import mode compresses all data into the VertiPaq in-memory columnar engine, so report queries are served entirely from RAM — generally producing the fastest interactive response times.
Shortcut mode is not a recognized Power BI semantic model storage mode; shortcuts are a OneLake storage feature, not a model storage setting.

questions.reference: [Semantic model modes in the Power BI service - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: Which pipeline design helps route failures to an alternate notification branch without stopping successful downstream paths unnecessarily?

options.text: Conditional paths based on activity outcome
options.explanation: Fabric data pipelines support conditional branching on activity outcomes (Success, Failure, Completion, Skipped), letting you route failed paths to an alerting step while successful paths continue to downstream activities.
Endorsement labels are metadata governance signals for content discoverability; they have no effect on pipeline execution branching.
Object-level security restricts which model objects users can see; it is not a pipeline orchestration concept.
Role-playing dimensions are a semantic data modeling pattern involving multiple relationships to the same dimension; they have nothing to do with pipeline error handling.

questions.reference: [Pipeline Overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: A semantic model design uses both imported dimensions and a live query path for detailed facts. Which semantic-model concept does this describe?

options.explanation: Shortcut mirroring is not a recognized Power BI semantic model pattern; it conflates two separate Fabric concepts.
The SQL analytics endpoint is a T-SQL query surface over lakehouse tables, not a description of how a model blends multiple storage modes.

A composite model allows different tables within the same semantic model to use different storage modes — for example, Import for small dimension tables and DirectQuery or DirectLake for large fact tables — enabling a flexible balance of performance and data freshness.
A gateway cluster is an infrastructure concept for high-availability connectivity to on-premises sources; it is not a semantic modeling pattern.

questions.reference: [Semantic model modes in the Power BI service - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: Which permission scope is generally broader: workspace roles or item-level sharing?

options.text: Item-level sharing is broader in all cases
options.explanation: Item-level sharing is more targeted — it grants access to a single specific artifact, making it narrower than a workspace role.

options.text: Workspace roles are broader because they govern access across items in the workspace
options.explanation: Workspace roles (Admin, Member, Contributor, Viewer) apply across all items in a workspace, giving the role holder permissions on every pipeline, lakehouse, report, and model in that workspace.
Workspace roles and item sharing are distinct mechanisms with different scopes; they are not identical controls.

options.text: Neither can grant access to reports
options.explanation: Both workspace roles and item-level sharing can be used to grant users access to reports in various read or build capacities.

questions.reference: [Roles in workspaces in Microsoft Fabric - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/fundamentals/roles-workspaces)

questions.stem: Which Fabric item is most aligned with SQL-centric dimensional warehousing and cross-database style analytical querying?

options.explanation: Notebooks are a code authoring and execution environment for Spark-based transformations and analysis; they are not a SQL warehousing experience.
Dataflows Gen2 are a low-code ETL tool for ingestion and transformation; they are not an analytical warehousing item.
Dashboards are a visualization surface for pinned tiles and reports; they do not provide SQL-centric warehousing capabilities.
The Fabric Data Warehouse is the SQL-first warehousing experience in Fabric, supporting T-SQL DDL/DML, dimensional modeling, and cross-database queries across warehouse and lakehouse objects.

questions.reference: [What Is Fabric Data Warehouse? - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-warehouse/data-warehousing)

questions.stem: Why would an analytics engineer create a calculation group in a semantic model?

options.explanation: Calculation groups are a DAX modeling construct; they do not move or mirror files in OneLake.
Column-level encryption is handled through external key management and not through calculation groups in the semantic model.
Gateway connectivity is configured in Power BI service data source settings and the gateway admin console, not through calculation groups.

options.text: To centralize reusable calculation logic such as time intelligence patterns
options.explanation: Calculation groups let an analytics engineer define a set of DAX calculation items — such as YTD, QTD, prior-period variance, and currency conversion — once in the model so they can be applied dynamically across any measure without duplicating formulas.

questions.reference: [Learn DAX basics in Power BI Desktop - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-quickstart-learn-dax-basics)

questions.stem: A pipeline copies data into a lakehouse and then triggers a notebook that expects the copy output path dynamically. What should the engineer use?

options.text: An activity output or parameter passed to the notebook
options.explanation: Fabric pipeline activities expose output properties (such as the copied file path or row count) that can be referenced in downstream activity parameters using dynamic content expressions, allowing the notebook to receive the runtime path without hardcoding it.
Endorsement labels are metadata governance signals (Promoted, Certified); they carry no runtime data and cannot pass values between pipeline activities.
Role-playing dimensions are a semantic data modeling pattern for reusing dimension tables in multiple relationships; they have no relevance to pipeline runtime parameter passing.
A DirectLake fallback rule is a query-execution behavior in semantic models, not a pipeline orchestration mechanism.

questions.reference: [Pipeline Overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: Which relationship setting most directly controls whether filters flow one way or in both directions between related tables?

options.explanation: The cross-filter direction property on a relationship determines whether filter context flows from the one-side to the many-side only (Single) or in both directions simultaneously (Both).
Incremental refresh is a partition management strategy for reducing model refresh time; it does not affect how filters propagate through relationships.
DirectLake framing governs how column segments are loaded from OneLake storage into the query engine cache; it has no bearing on relationship filter propagation.
Object-level security controls which tables and columns are visible to users or roles; it does not govern filter direction between related tables.

questions.reference: [Semantic model modes in the Power BI service - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: If capacity usage is persistently throttled during peak business hours, what is the most appropriate first response?

options.explanation: Security roles (RLS, OLS, workspace roles) have negligible effect on capacity CU consumption; removing them does not resolve throttling and introduces a serious security risk.

options.text: Convert every model to DirectQuery immediately
options.explanation: Converting all Import models to DirectQuery shifts query processing to source systems and can actually increase latency and source load, worsening end-user experience without addressing capacity root causes.
The SQL analytics endpoint is a lightweight query surface; removing it does not free significant capacity and cuts off analyst access.

options.text: Review Capacity Metrics and right-size or reschedule workloads
options.explanation: The first step is to examine the Capacity Metrics app to understand which workloads are consuming the most CUs, then take evidence-based action such as upscaling capacity, staggering refresh schedules, or pausing lower-priority jobs during peak hours.

questions.reference: [What is the Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app? - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/enterprise/metrics-app)

questions.stem: Which Fabric control determines whether a user can open and edit content across many items in a workspace?

options.explanation: Workspace roles (Admin, Member, Contributor, Viewer) define what actions a user can perform across the entire workspace, including whether they can view, edit, publish, or manage all items in that workspace.
V-order is a storage write optimization applied to Delta/parquet files to improve read performance for the Power BI engine; it is not a security or access control.
Incremental refresh is a semantic model refresh strategy that limits which date-range partitions are processed during each refresh cycle; it has no access control function.
Framing is a DirectLake internal process for loading column data from OneLake into the in-memory cache; it has nothing to do with workspace access permissions.

questions.reference: [Roles in workspaces in Microsoft Fabric - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/fundamentals/roles-workspaces)

questions.stem: A business user wants a trusted certified model for finance and a less-governed exploratory model for ad hoc work. What should the team do?

options.text: Use one model only because endorsement must be uniform
options.explanation: Endorsement does not need to be uniform — different items in the same workspace can carry different endorsement states, and it is common to have some certified and some unendorsed items.

options.text: Turn off RLS on the exploratory model
options.explanation: Removing RLS from the exploratory model is an unrelated security decision and does not address the governance discoverability goal of the scenario.
There is no reason to host semantic models outside Fabric; Fabric is the appropriate platform and moving models out would reduce governance, discoverability, and integration.

options.text: Apply different endorsement states to the two models
options.explanation: Endorsement labels are applied per-item, so the finance model can carry the Certified label while the exploratory model remains unendorsed or Promoted, clearly communicating the different trust levels to business users.

questions.reference: [Promoted and certified items](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-endorsement-overview)

questions.stem: Why would a team use a OneLake shortcut instead of copying data into a new lakehouse?

options.text: To convert the data into DAX measures automatically
options.explanation: Shortcuts do not generate DAX; they are a storage virtualization feature that exposes data at a OneLake path without defining any business logic.

options.text: To guarantee Import mode in Power BI
options.explanation: A shortcut does not dictate which Power BI storage mode is used; a model reading from shortcut-exposed data can still be Import, DirectLake, or DirectQuery.
Source-system permissions still apply to a shortcut; Fabric must be authorized to read from the target location, and workspace users still need appropriate OneLake permissions.

options.text: To reduce data duplication while keeping a single governed access path
options.explanation: A shortcut creates a virtual reference to the original data location so multiple teams or workspaces can access the same dataset without each maintaining a separate physical copy, reducing storage cost and keeping a single source of truth.

questions.reference: [Unify data sources with OneLake shortcuts - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-shortcuts)

questions.stem: Which feature helps monitor historical pipeline runs and diagnose which activity failed?

options.explanation: The pipeline run history view shows all past executions with their overall status, per-activity duration and outcome, and error messages, giving engineers the information they need to troubleshoot failures.
OneLake shortcuts are a storage virtualization feature; they do not track or expose pipeline execution history.
Calculation groups are DAX semantic model constructs for managing time intelligence and other reusable calculations; they have no relation to pipeline monitoring.
Object-level security is a semantic model access control that hides tables or columns from unauthorized users; it does not provide pipeline execution diagnostics.

questions.reference: [Pipeline Overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: Which DAX artifact returns a value based on the current filter context rather than storing one result per row?

options.explanation: Measures are DAX expressions evaluated dynamically at query time in response to the current filter context produced by slicers, visuals, and relationships — they produce a single scalar result per context, not a stored row-level value.
Calculated columns are evaluated once per row during table refresh and stored persistently in the model's columnar cache; they are row-context, not filter-context, expressions.
A OneLake shortcut is a storage virtualization feature that creates a virtual path to data in another location; it is not a DAX artifact.
An on-premises data gateway is a connectivity component that bridges private networks to cloud services; it is not related to DAX calculation types.

questions.reference: [Learn DAX basics in Power BI Desktop - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-quickstart-learn-dax-basics)

questions.stem: When should item-level sharing be preferred over granting a broad workspace role?

options.text: When access should be limited to a specific report or model
options.explanation: Item-level sharing grants access only to that individual report or model, following least-privilege principles when broader workspace access is unnecessary and would expose other items the user should not see.

options.text: When users need full administration rights
options.explanation: Full administration rights require the workspace Admin role, not item-level sharing; item-level sharing does not confer any administrative capabilities.
Resizing capacity is an administrative action performed by a Fabric capacity administrator in the Azure portal or Fabric admin center; it is unrelated to workspace or item permissions.
Running VACUUM on a Delta table is a data maintenance operation performed inside a notebook or via T-SQL; it has no connection to how access is granted to workspace items.

questions.reference: [Roles in workspaces in Microsoft Fabric - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/fundamentals/roles-workspaces)

questions.stem: A semantic model uses incremental refresh with change detection. What additional benefit does change detection provide?

options.text: It can avoid refreshing partitions whose tracked change column has not changed
options.explanation: Change detection uses a polling expression against a source column (such as a max-modified timestamp) to determine whether data in a partition has actually changed; if it has not, the partition refresh is skipped entirely, further reducing processing time beyond what incremental refresh alone achieves.
Change detection does not disable partition pruning; pruning continues to operate and change detection adds an extra skip condition on top of it.

options.text: It replaces the date range policy
options.explanation: Change detection complements the date-range partition policy — it works within the policy to decide whether in-scope partitions actually need refreshing; it does not replace the policy.
Incremental refresh and change detection apply to Import-mode models and have no connection to DirectLake storage mode.

questions.reference: [Configure incremental refresh and real-time data for Power BI semantic models - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/incremental-refresh-overview)

questions.stem: Which Fabric feature is most appropriate for low-code ingestion from many SaaS and database sources into a repeatable workflow?

options.explanation: The Copy activity in Fabric data pipelines provides a wizard-driven connector library covering hundreds of SaaS APIs and database systems, making it the go-to low-code ingestion primitive for repeatable batch workflows.
Object-level security is a semantic model access control that restricts visibility of tables and columns; it does not move or load data from external sources.
Role-playing dimensions are a data modeling pattern for reusing dimension tables in multiple relationship roles within a semantic model; they are not an ingestion tool.
DirectLake fallback is a runtime query behavior in which a DirectLake semantic model reverts to DirectQuery execution; it is not related to data ingestion.

questions.reference: [Pipeline Overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: A DirectLake model shows acceptable report latency but high memory pressure. Which optimization is most likely relevant before rewriting the model?

options.explanation: Workspace roles are a permissions construct; removing them does not meaningfully reduce the memory that DirectLake loads when serving queries.

Converting shortcuts to CSV exports would worsen data freshness and governance without addressing the root cause of memory pressure, which relates to what columns and rows are loaded into the VertiPaq cache.
Endorsement is a discoverability label; it provides no security enforcement and cannot substitute for RLS, nor does it affect memory consumption.

options.text: Review framing behavior and model design to reduce unnecessary columns
options.explanation: DirectLake memory pressure often stems from framing in too many columns or having high-cardinality columns that the model does not need; auditing the model to remove unused columns and reviewing which columns trigger framing is the appropriate first optimization step.

questions.reference: [Direct Lake overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/directlake-overview)

questions.stem: Which statement about lakehouse maintenance is accurate in Fabric?

options.text: Engineers can run optimization and cleanup tasks such as `OPTIMIZE` and `VACUUM` on Delta tables
options.explanation: Fabric lakehouses fully support Delta table maintenance operations — engineers can run OPTIMIZE to compact small files into larger ones and VACUUM to remove files no longer referenced by the Delta transaction log.

options.text: Lakehouses do not support table maintenance
options.explanation: Fabric lakehouses actively support Delta maintenance; engineers are encouraged to run these operations periodically for query performance.

options.text: Only the Capacity Metrics app can manage Delta files
options.explanation: The Capacity Metrics app is a monitoring tool for CU consumption; it has no capability to run Delta maintenance commands on tables.

options.text: Maintenance requires converting the lakehouse to a warehouse
options.explanation: Delta maintenance operations are available natively in a Lakehouse via notebooks or the SQL analytics endpoint; no conversion to a Warehouse is required.

questions.reference: [What is a lakehouse? - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview)

questions.stem: Which outcome is most likely if a semantic model is not governed and every report duplicates its own business logic?

options.explanation: When each report team independently defines its own version of measures like revenue or margin, small definitional differences accumulate into visible discrepancies — executives see different numbers in different reports for the same time period, eroding trust in the data.
Duplicating logic in multiple report models typically consumes more capacity through redundant model refreshes and redundant in-memory storage, not less.
Decentralized models mean each team maintains its own RLS and OLS, making it harder to apply consistent security, not easier to reuse it.
Deploying many independent models through deployment pipelines is more complex than deploying a single governed shared model.

questions.reference: [Semantic model modes in the Power BI service - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: Which storage mode is typically chosen when the source must always be queried live and data should not be imported into the model cache?

options.explanation: DirectQuery passes every query through to the underlying data source at runtime, ensuring the report always reflects the current state of the source without any cached copy in the model.
Import mode loads a copy of the data into the model's VertiPaq cache — the opposite of the requirement to avoid importing.
DirectLake is a storage mode specific to Fabric/Power BI that reads parquet files from OneLake; it is not a general-purpose "always query the source live" mode for arbitrary external databases.
Role-playing is a dimensional modeling pattern (reusing a dimension in multiple relationships) and is not a recognized Power BI storage mode.

questions.reference: [Semantic model modes in the Power BI service - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: A user has access to a workspace but still cannot see a secured table field in a report built from a semantic model. What is the most likely explanation?

options.text: Object-level security hides the field even though workspace access exists
options.explanation: OLS is enforced at the semantic model layer independently of workspace roles — a user who can open the workspace and view the report will still see a blank or absent field if OLS is restricting that column or table for their identity or role.

options.text: OneLake shortcut latency is masking the column
options.explanation: Shortcut latency is a performance concern (slow data access), not a visibility issue that would cause a column to disappear from a report for security reasons.

options.text: Incremental refresh permanently removed the column
options.explanation: Incremental refresh manages which data partitions are loaded during refresh; it does not permanently remove columns from the model schema.

options.text: The Capacity Metrics app is blocking access
options.explanation: The Capacity Metrics app is a monitoring tool for CU usage; it has no role in enforcing or blocking semantic model security.

questions.reference: [Object-Level Security (OLS) with Power BI - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/security/service-admin-object-level-security)

questions.stem: Why would an engineer add email or alerting steps after critical pipeline branches?

options.text: To make operational failures visible to the support team quickly
options.explanation: Adding notification steps on failure branches reduces mean time to awareness (MTTA), enabling on-call engineers or support teams to begin investigation before the issue cascades into downstream reports or SLA breaches.
Email and alerting activities are pipeline communication steps; they have no effect on how the DAX engine evaluates expressions in a semantic model.
OneLake shortcuts are created through the Fabric workspace UI or API; they cannot be created as a side-effect of a pipeline notification activity.
Semantic model certification is an endorsement action performed by authorized users in the workspace settings; it is not triggered by a pipeline activity.

questions.reference: [Pipeline Overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: Which Fabric workload is most directly associated with notebooks and Spark-based engineering over Delta data?

options.explanation: The Data Engineering experience in Microsoft Fabric is the dedicated Spark workload, providing notebooks, Spark job definitions, and lakehouses for code-based engineering over Delta data at scale.
Power BI app publishing is a BI distribution task performed in the Power BI service; it belongs to the Power BI experience, not Data Engineering.
Capacity metrics monitoring is performed through the Fabric Capacity Metrics app, which is an administrative monitoring tool, not a data engineering workload.
Endorsement management (applying Promoted or Certified labels) is a governance action in the Power BI or Fabric workspace settings; it is not a Spark or Data Engineering workload.

questions.reference: [What is a lakehouse? - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview)

questions.stem: A finance report requires both row filtering by region and hiding a sensitive compensation column. Which TWO controls are needed? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Row-level security uses DAX role predicates to restrict which rows each user or role can see — in this case, filtering Sales rows to the user's own region.
Object-level security restricts access to specific tables or columns, so the sensitive compensation column can be hidden from report consumers who are not in an authorized role.
Endorsement labels (Promoted, Certified) signal curation quality to users browsing the data hub; they do not enforce any row-level or column-level data access restriction.
Deployment pipelines manage the promotion of Fabric content between dev, test, and production workspace stages; they are a release management tool, not a data security control.

questions.reference: [Object-Level Security (OLS) with Power BI - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/security/service-admin-object-level-security)

questions.stem: Which TWO Fabric capabilities best support a medallion-style analytics workflow that needs ingestion orchestration and low-code cleansing? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Data pipelines orchestrate the sequence of ingestion, transformation, and delivery activities across medallion layers, including triggering notebooks, copy operations, and dataflows in the correct order.
Dataflows Gen2 provide a visual Power Query editor for low-code cleansing and shaping — ideal for the silver-layer transformations that prepare raw bronze data for curated gold tables.
Endorsement labels (Promoted, Certified) are governance metadata for content discoverability; they do not execute any ETL logic or move data between medallion layers.
The Capacity Metrics app is an operational monitoring tool for tracking CU consumption and throttling; it does not implement or orchestrate data transformations.

questions.reference: [Differences between Dataflow Gen1 and Dataflow Gen2 - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about DirectLake are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: It can read OneLake-backed data without a full import
options.explanation: DirectLake reads column data directly from OneLake-backed Delta/parquet files on demand without requiring a scheduled import; data is framed into the VertiPaq cache as needed per query.

options.text: It may fall back to DirectQuery in some scenarios
options.explanation: When a query hits a DirectLake guardrail condition — such as a feature or function not supported in native DirectLake execution — Fabric automatically falls back to DirectQuery mode for that query.

options.text: It always requires a gateway to query OneLake files
options.explanation: DirectLake reads from OneLake, which is a native Fabric cloud storage service; no on-premises data gateway is needed to access it.

options.text: It is identical to classic Import mode
options.explanation: DirectLake is architecturally distinct from Import mode — Import copies all data into VertiPaq at refresh time, while DirectLake reads selectively from storage at query time and does not require a scheduled refresh cycle.

questions.reference: [Direct Lake overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/directlake-overview)

questions.stem: Which TWO actions most improve discoverability and trust of enterprise-curated analytics assets? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: The Certified endorsement label signals to data hub users that the item has been reviewed and approved by authorized data stewards, making it easy to identify trustworthy content.

A shared semantic model published to a workspace enables self-service report creation against a single governed set of measures and relationships, improving reuse and consistency across the organization.
Disabling row-level security reduces data protection and can expose sensitive information to unauthorized users; it directly harms governance rather than improving it.

options.text: Force every report to use private dataflows
options.explanation: Requiring each report team to maintain private isolated dataflows fragments data preparation, reduces reuse, and makes the overall analytics environment harder to govern and discover.

questions.reference: [Promoted and certified items](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-endorsement-overview)

questions.stem: Why would an analytics engineer choose a Warehouse instead of a Lakehouse for a team of SQL analysts?

options.text: Warehouses are the only items that can store data in Fabric
options.explanation: Many Fabric item types store data in OneLake — lakehouses, warehouses, eventhouses, and more; warehouses are not the only storage option.

options.text: Lakehouses cannot be queried with SQL at all
options.explanation: Lakehouses do expose a SQL analytics endpoint for T-SQL querying; the difference is that it is read-only, whereas the Warehouse provides full T-SQL DDL/DML.
Data Warehouses in Fabric support governance features including workspace roles, endorsement, and integration with Purview.

options.text: The Warehouse provides a SQL-first experience for dimensional analytics
options.explanation: The Fabric Data Warehouse is designed as a SQL-first experience with support for T-SQL CREATE TABLE, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, stored procedures, and cross-database queries — making it the natural choice when a SQL analyst team needs a full warehousing workflow.

questions.reference: [What Is Fabric Data Warehouse? - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-warehouse/data-warehousing)

questions.stem: A semantic model contains reusable business calculations that should appear consistently across many reports. Where should those calculations live?

options.explanation: Model-level measures are defined once in the semantic model and are available to every report that connects to it, ensuring that the same Revenue, Margin, or YTD Sales calculation is used consistently across the organization.
Visual calculations are scoped to a single visual in a single report and cannot be reused by other reports or even other visuals in the same report.
OneLake shortcuts are a storage virtualization feature; they expose data paths but cannot contain DAX business logic or calculations.
Workspace roles (Admin, Member, Contributor, Viewer) are access-control assignments; they cannot hold DAX expressions or business calculation definitions.

questions.reference: [Learn DAX basics in Power BI Desktop - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-quickstart-learn-dax-basics)

questions.stem: Which Fabric feature is best for low-code reshaping of source data before it lands in curated analytical assets?

options.explanation: Dataflows Gen2 use the Power Query visual editor to let analysts and engineers reshape, filter, and merge data from many sources without writing Spark or SQL code, then land the results in a lakehouse or warehouse.
The Capacity Metrics app monitors CU consumption and throttling; it does not transform or reshape source data.
Endorsement labels are governance metadata used to communicate trust and curation quality; they do not perform any data transformation.
Role-playing dimensions are a semantic data modeling pattern for reusing a single dimension in multiple relationship roles; they are a model design concept, not a data ingestion or reshaping tool.

questions.reference: [Differences between Dataflow Gen1 and Dataflow Gen2 - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: What is the main benefit of publishing reports against a shared semantic model rather than separate private models?

options.explanation: When multiple reports share a single semantic model, all reports use the same measure definitions, relationships, and security rules — reducing metric disagreements between reports and making updates to business logic a one-place change.

options.text: Guaranteed lower capacity usage in every case
options.explanation: Capacity impact depends on the size of the model and query patterns; sharing a model does not guarantee lower capacity usage in all scenarios.
Security settings (RLS, OLS, workspace roles) remain active and important regardless of whether the model is shared; sharing a model does not waive security requirements.

options.text: It eliminates the need for relationships in the model
options.explanation: Relationships between tables are still defined and essential in a shared semantic model; sharing the model across reports does not remove the need for proper dimensional modeling.

questions.reference: [Semantic model modes in the Power BI service - Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-dataset-modes-understand)

questions.stem: Why should an engineer investigate guardrail-triggered DirectLake fallbacks?

options.explanation: A DirectLake fallback is a transparent query-path switch; it does not delete, invalidate, or alter the semantic model definition in any way.

Falling back from DirectLake to DirectQuery typically increases query latency because queries are then translated and sent to the underlying source rather than reading pre-framed columnar data, which generally means worse, not better, performance.
DirectLake fallbacks do not modify security definitions; RLS roles remain active and are enforced even in DirectQuery fallback mode.

options.text: Fallbacks can change performance characteristics and source-query behavior
options.explanation: When queries fall back to DirectQuery, they are routed through the OneLake SQL endpoint rather than reading native parquet files, which can change response times, concurrency behavior, and source query load in ways the engineer should understand and address.

questions.reference: [Direct Lake overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/enterprise/directlake-overview)

questions.stem: Which approach best balances trust signaling and access control for a curated finance model?

options.text: Certify the model and remove all security filters
options.explanation: Removing all security filters from a certified finance model would expose sensitive financial data (such as compensation, forecasts, or unreleased results) to anyone who can access the model, defeating the purpose of certification.

options.text: Rely only on workspace roles and avoid the semantic model
options.explanation: Workspace roles are too coarse for finance scenarios — they grant access to all items in a workspace and cannot restrict which rows or columns within a semantic model a given user can see.

options.text: Use DirectLake fallback as the main control
options.explanation: DirectLake fallback is a runtime query-execution behavior that activates when a guardrail is hit; it is not a security mechanism and provides no access control.

options.text: Certify the model and apply RLS/OLS as needed
options.explanation: Applying the Certified endorsement label communicates trustworthiness and curation to business users, while properly configured RLS and OLS ensure that each user sees only the rows and columns appropriate for their role — delivering both discoverability and data protection.

questions.reference: [Promoted and certified items](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-endorsement-overview)
